name: Paska
recipeInstructions:
1. Pass 3 pounds of dry pot cheese through a very fine sieve and blend it well.
2. Beat 8 egg yolks with 1-1/2 pounds of granulated sugar until smooth.
3. Combine the egg yolk mixture with the blended cheese.
4. Add the heavy cream, chopped almonds, mixed candied fruits, raisins, and scraped vanilla seeds to the mixture. Mix until the fruits are evenly distributed.
5. Line a pyramidal paskha form with cheesecloth wrung out of cold water.
6. Pour the mixture into the form and fold the cheesecloth to cover the bottom of the cake.
7. Weight the cheese down well and allow it to drip for 24 hours.
8. Use a deep flowerpot with a hole in the bottom for drainage as a mold if desired.
9. Unmold the paskha and decorate as you wish.
10. Serve traditionally with kulich, the Russian Easter bread.
